title: Full Zone Processing
brief: Build full main, processor, crossover, delay, and fill zone routing.

puzzle.scenario: You are routing a full-zone processing rig: Main L/R through system processing and crossover, Bus 1 to a delay tower chain, and Bus 2 to a front-fill chain.
puzzle.objective: Patch Main L/R through the system processor and crossover to the high, mid, and low amp inputs, route Bus 1 through the delay tower processor to the stereo delay amp, and route Bus 2 through the front-fill processor to the fill amp.

constraints.text: Main L/R, Bus 1, and Bus 2 serve different zone-processing paths and should not be collapsed into one generic PA output.

puzzle.completionExplanation: The completed patch sends Main L/R through system processing, crossover, and high/mid/low amplifier inputs; sends Bus 1 through delay processing to the stereo delay amp; and sends Bus 2 through front-fill processing to the fill amp.

educationalFeedback.defaultWrongRoute: This locked board accepts wrong distinct liv026-* attempts as non-completing invalid red routes for feedback; only the required route list counts toward completion.

stereoGroups.purpose: Main L/R outputs feed the system processor inputs in left/right order.
System processor L/R outputs feed the 3-way crossover inputs.
Crossover high-band L/R outputs feed the high amplifier inputs.
Crossover mid-band L/R outputs feed the mid amplifier inputs.
Crossover low-band L/R outputs feed the low amplifier inputs.
Delay processor L/R outputs feed the stereo delay amplifier inputs.

broadInvalidRouteRule.rule: When a pair is not a valid route, any two distinct node keys starting with liv026- are allowed to draw an invalid red route.
broadInvalidRouteRule.invalidRouteKeyShape: invalid:<sorted liv026 endpoint keys joined with -->

notes: The launch block contains no explicit forbidden pairs for LIV-026.
Wrong-route behavior is renderer-specific today and is not represented as canonical puzzle trapRoutes.
Future manifest conversion must preserve broad invalid-route red-cable behavior without counting false hitboxes or invalid routes toward completion.

falseHitboxBehavior.invalidRouteRendering: False hitboxes participate in broad LIV-026 invalid-route rendering but do not count toward completion.
